key_findings: Total population: 4,240 approved expense records (2025), 80 employees, 10 departments, 4,234 unique invoices. All records have status='approved', special_approval=0, budget_year=2025.

Rule 1 (重复报销): 6 duplicate invoice groups identified. Each of 6 invoices (FP202500000002, 005, 020, 028, 037, 055) was used in 2 reimbursement records. 12 records involved total. Original records (R000002-R000055) and duplicate records (R004201-R004206).

Rule 2 (拆分报销): 78 split reimbursement groups found using greedy non-overlapping sliding window algorithm (7-day window, consecutive records with date_diff <= 6 days, combined >= 3000). 70 groups are training_fee, 8 groups are travel_lodging. 168 unique records involved.

Rule 3 (超标准): 5 records exceed policy standards: R004221 (office_supplies 650 > 600), R004222 (communication 330 > 300), R004223 (training_fee 3700 > 3500), R004224 (business_entertainment 350/person > 300), R004225 (travel_lodging 900/night > 850 for D1 tier A).
Rule 4 (超预算): 6 departments exceed annual budget: D001 投资银行部, D002 固定收益部, D003 财富管理部, D004 研究所, D005 机构业务部, D006 运营管理部. 4 departments (D007-D010) remain within budget.
Rule 5 (超期报销): 6 records with reimbursement delay > 60 days, ranging from 65 to 120 days. All are communication expense type from extra test records (R004227-R004232).
Total: 101 anomalies (6 + 78 + 5 + 6 + 6) involving 197 unique records across all rules. No overlap between rule populations.
unresolved_items: Rule 2 split reimbursement window algorithm: used greedy non-overlapping sliding window approach (78 findings). Alternative overlapping-window approach yields 96 findings. The non-overlapping interpretation is more conservative but may miss some edge cases where overlapping windows are valid per policy.

Training fee records have no nights/days/participants populated for main records (R000001-R004200), so per-night accommodation and comprehensive training sub-rules could not be applied to those records. Only test record R004223 triggered the training fee standard check.
The 40 extra records (R004201-R004240) appear to be synthetic test cases deliberately designed to trigger rule violations. They are included in the analysis per the 'in-scope population' directive.
